title: Deschampsia laevigata
taxonomy: Deschampsia laevigata is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Deschampsia, which is a group of perennial and annual grasses. The Poaceae family is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous flowering plants that include grasses and other related species. Deschampsia laevigata is native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its classification also includes being a part of the tribe Poeae, which consists of grasses with two-flowered spikelets.

properties: Deschampsia laevigata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of various respiratory issues, including bronchitis and coughs. intro: Deschampsia laevigata, also known as smooth hair grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce symptoms of respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
